# Figure description: Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T1.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Схема_изоглубин_по_уровню_T1.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isodepth map** (схема изоглубин) — a contour map showing the depth to a key seismic reflector horizon across a regional area. It is a type of structural-seismic isoline map, combining depth contours (isoglubiny/изоглубины) with interpreted fault traces, zone boundaries, and surface outcrop indicators. The map is presented at a scale of 1:500,000.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large area in **northern Kazakhstan** corresponding to the **Тенизская впадина (Teniz Depression/Basin)** and surrounding regions, compiled from seismic survey data. It is drawn to the reference reflector horizon designated **T₁** (Опорный отражающий горизонт T₁ — the key/reference seismic reflector horizon T₁). The sheet layout inset (Схема расположения листов) shows the map covers multiple sub-sheets arranged in a grid, including at minimum sheets labeled I–XV and rows XXXIV–XXXV, consistent with 1:200,000 map sheet coverage across the M-42/N-42 area. The upper-right inset panel is explicitly referenced as: **"К отчету Залайне-Тенизской партии за 1978–84 гг."** (pertaining to the report of the Zalayne-Teniz party for 1978–84). The **Чертёж №42** (Drawing No. 42) designation appears in the upper right.

---

## Key Content

**Depth Contours (Изоглубины):**
- The map is covered by closely spaced contour lines representing equal depth to the T₁ seismic horizon. Contour values visible include figures in the range of approximately **1000–4000+ metres**, with some labels reading values such as 1500, 2000, 2500, 3000 m and deeper. Contour spacing is variable, reflecting both gentle structural limbs and steep structural gradients.
- In the central and southern portions of the map, contours show a broad, deep **basin or depression structure** (the Teniz Depression), where depths to T₁ are greatest (deepest colours and tightest contours).
- In the western and northwestern portions, contours show shallower depths and more irregular, anticlinal-type closures, suggesting uplifted structural highs or basement ridges.

**Colour Scheme (Шкала интенсивности изоглубин):**
- A **pink-to-red shading** scheme is used. The legend scale bar (Шкала интенсивности изоглубин 5 м) shows gradation from white (shallow, ~0–1000 m) through light pink to deeper red (>4000 m). The deeper parts of the Teniz Depression are shaded in moderate to strong pink/red, concentrated in the central and southeastern parts of the map.
- The pinkish anomaly areas are irregular in shape, broadly following the basin axis trending roughly NW–SE.

**Structural and Tectonic Features:**
- **Fault traces** (Разрывные нарушения, предполагаемые по сейсмозондировочным данным — inferred faults from seismic sounding data) are shown as dashed lines with tick marks. Multiple fault lineaments cross the map, particularly prominent in the central and southwestern sectors, forming a network of NW–SE and NE–SW trending structures.
- Several **anticlinal closures** are visible as concentric closed contours, particularly in the western portion of the map, where tighter contour loops indicate local structural highs — potential hydrocarbon or mineral exploration targets.
- **Зоны потери корреляции отражённых волн** (zones of correlation loss of reflected waves), predominantly associated with faulted or steeply dipping zones, are shown with hatched diagonal symbols. These appear in the upper-right inset area and in parts of the main map, indicating areas of complex subsurface geometry where seismic data quality is poor.
- **Выходы на поверхность отложений варийско-ской свиты и более древних пород по сейсмическим данным** (outcrops of Variscan-suite and older rock formations at surface, per seismic data) are shown with a dense cross-hatched pattern. These appear in the upper-right inset, representing basement outcrops or near-surface ancient rocks.
- **Выходы на поверхность отложений варийско-ской свиты и более древних пород по сейсмозондировочным данным** (similar outcrops per seismic sounding data) are shown with a different cross-hatch pattern.
- **Граница Тенизской впадины** (boundary of the Teniz Depression) is marked as a distinctive boundary line separating the depressed basin interior from surrounding platform areas.

**Index Map (Схема расположения листов):**
- A small sheet-layout diagram in the lower-right shows the spatial arrangement of constituent map sheets (labeled with Roman numerals I through approximately XV, and with sheet rows XXXIV–XXXV at top), clarifying how this compiled map relates to the standard 1:200,000 topographic/geological sheet grid.
